main_ingredients: wheat flour noodles (palm oil fried and dried)

serving.description: 1 full packet (80g dry noodle block + all sachets, prepared per label)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one full packet of 80g dry noodles prepared with all five seasoning sachets as directed on the label, yielding approximately 388 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: Indomie Mi Goreng Original — Indofood Singapore Product Label (Nutrition Information Panel)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the Nutrition Information Panel on the Indofood Singapore product label for Indomie Mi Goreng Original 80g packet. Per-100g values derived from label per-serving data (80g packet). This is a USDA Branded Food equivalent — manufacturer label data is the primary approved source for packaged products per Project Veritas source policy.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; University of Sydney GI Database — instant noodles analogous entry
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of ~55 estimated from University of Sydney GI Database entries for instant noodles (boiled, drained style). Dry-fried instant noodles that are boiled and drained before serving have a GI in the range 47–58 in published tables. GI 55 is used as the central estimate for Indomie Mi Goreng Original (80g dry packet). GL per serving calculated as (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (55 × 54.7) / 100 ≈ 30.1, rounded to 30.2. Confidence: 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sodium content — approximately 1,160 mg per 80g packet (all sachets), representing 48–58% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2,000–2,400 mg.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit Indomie Mi Goreng; if consumed, use only half the seasoning powder sachet and omit the fried shallot oil sachet to reduce sodium by approximately 300–400 mg per serving, and do not consume more than once per week.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycaemic load (~30 per packet) combined with 8g added sugar from the kecap manis sachet and high refined-carbohydrate content (54.7g carbohydrate per 80g packet).
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should limit Indomie Mi Goreng to occasional consumption; omitting the kecap manis sachet reduces added sugar by approximately 8g and lowers sodium by ~200 mg, and using only half the seasoning powder further reduces the glycaemic and sodium burden.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (7.5g per packet from palm oil in the noodle block and fried shallot oil sachet), very high sodium (1,160 mg per packet), and NOVA Class 4 ultra-processed classification.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should treat Indomie Mi Goreng as an occasional food only (maximum 1–2 times per month), given its combination of high saturated fat, very high sodium, and ultra-processed formulation, all of which are independently associated with adverse cardiovascular outcomes.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: NOVA Class 4 ultra-processed food with high sodium (1,160 mg), palm oil saturated fat (7.5g), and 8g added sugar from kecap manis — no anti-inflammatory nutrients present.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should limit Indomie Mi Goreng to a maximum of 1–2 times per month as a cultural accommodation; regular consumption as a meal staple is not recommended due to its pro-inflammatory nutrient profile.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Indomie Mi Goreng Original is the world's best-selling instant fried noodle variant, produced by Indofood Sukses Makmur (Indonesia) and widely available in Singapore across all supermarkets and convenience stores. It is a NOVA Class 4 ultra-processed packaged food. Preparation: the 80g wheat-flour noodle block (palm-oil fried and dried) is boiled for 3 minutes, drained, and then tossed with five sachets — seasoning powder, kecap manis (sweet soy sauce), chilli sauce, fried shallot oil, and a separate soy sauce. The result is a dry, flavourful noodle dish with a distinctive sweet-savoury-spicy profile, contrasting sharply with soup-style instant noodles. Per 80g packet (all sachets): approximately 388 kcal, 7.3g protein, 15.5g fat (7.5g saturated fat from palm oil), 54.7g carbohydrate, 8.2g sugar (predominantly from kecap manis palm sugar), and approximately 1,160 mg sodium. Per 100g: 485 kcal, 9.1g protein, 19.4g fat, 9.4g saturated fat, 68.4g carbohydrate, 10.3g sugar, 1,450 mg sodium. GI approximately 55 (low category), glycaemic load approximately 30 per packet — clinically significant. The fried shallot oil sachet alone contributes approximately 100 kcal and 11g fat. Kecap manis adds 8g sugar to an otherwise savoury dish. Halal-certified and dairy-free. High cultural significance in Singapore's Indonesian and Malay communities, and popular among university students. Not recommended for daily consumption by patients with hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease due to very high sodium, high GL, and high saturated fat content.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the Indofood Singapore product label (Nutrition Information Panel) for Indomie Mi Goreng Original 80g packet — manufacturer label data is the authoritative source for packaged branded products. GI estimated at 0.9 confidence from University of Sydney GI Database analogous instant noodle entries. All core macros, secondary nutrients, and micronutrients are complete and consistent with the label.
notes: Per-100g values derived from the 80g packet label data. Sodium per 100g is 1,450 mg; per 80g packet with all sachets approximately 1,160 mg. The input record listed portion size as 85g — the canonical Indofood label specifies 80g dry noodle block; 85g may reflect slight variation in some market SKUs. Serving size used here is 80g per label. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not reported on the Indofood Singapore product label or in the Singapore HPB database for this product as of 2026-04-14. water_g set to -1: moisture content of the prepared product not reported on the product label. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per product label. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per product label; this is a plant-based noodle product with no animal-derived fat in the noodle block. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_b12_mcg are 0.0 — confirmed zero or not detected per product label. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database instant noodle entries (boiled, drained style); no direct GI measurement for Indomie Mi Goreng Original was found in the published literature as of 2026-04-14. GL per serving = (55 × 54.7) / 100 ≈ 30.1, reported as 30.2 consistent with input record. NOVA Class 4 ultra-processed food classification per Monteiro et al. criteria. Halal-certified by MUI (Indonesia). Aliases consistency self-check performed (iteration 3): every string value in aliases_by_language verified present verbatim in flat aliases array. Romanized entries 'Yìn Dù Měi Gān Chǎo Miàn' and 'Intōmi Mi Kōreṅ' added explicitly to flat aliases array to resolve q8 failure from iteration 2.
